title: "Explainer-to-article" reusable templates for newsrooms
context: Journalists and health/data newsroom staff are a key beneficiary segment: they need accurate, attributable, reusable screening trend figures and explanation they can quote and build articles from. Reusable templates that show journalists how to correctly use the dataset and explainers — with proper attribution, correct statistical framing, and the 'not medical advice' guardrails respected in editorial context — reduce the risk of misreporting. These templates are medium risk (not patient-facing in the same direct way as the explainers, but health journalism influences public health behavior). They are education-only reference materials for journalists, not advice to patients.
objective: Produce a set of reusable 'explainer-to-article' templates for newsrooms and journalists: showing how to correctly cite and use the screening-coverage-trends dataset and explainers in health journalism, with proper attribution, correct statistical framing, 'not medical advice' guardrail respected in editorial context, and provenance cited.
acceptanceCriteria: Templates are framed as education-only reference materials for journalists; they explain how to use the dataset and explainers correctly in news articles, not how to give health advice to readers

Templates include: how to cite the dataset with correct attribution (CC-BY-4.0), how to describe screening coverage rates accurately (denominator, basis, uncertainty), how to avoid common misstatements (causal claims from trends, omitting denominator, not flagging self-report basis)

Templates include guidance on the 'not medical advice' guardrail in editorial context: recommending that newsroom articles include a note directing readers to their care provider for personal health decisions, consistent with responsible health journalism
Every figure example in the templates is provenance-cited to the screening-coverage-trends dataset with the correct source attribution chain (dataset -> source agency)
Templates do not reproduce or paraphrase NC/SA sources (WHO GHO, IARC/GLOBOCAN) as if they were open; only open licensed figures from the dataset are used in examples
Templates are factually reviewed for statistical accuracy: no example misrepresents a screening statistic; statistical reviewer sign-off recorded before publication
output: A published set of 'explainer-to-article' newsroom templates: correct attribution and statistical framing guidance, 'not medical advice' guardrail in editorial context, provenance-cited figure examples from the open dataset only; statistical reviewer sign-off recorded.
